rulings.Case Title: FUND RECOVERY SERVICES, INC., A CA. CORP. VS. LIBERT MYERS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Injunctive And Declatory Relief; Notice
rulings.Rulings: Law and Motion Calendar for, Wednesday, October 15, 2014, line 1. Defendant Libert Myers' Motion For Injunctive And Declatory ReliefContinued to November 14, 2014 to give moving party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Moving party must deliver courtesy copies of the moving papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than October 15, 2014 at 4:00 pm or the motion will be denied. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the opposition papers. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: SCHAPIRO-THORN INC., VS. DANIEL L. MITCHELL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Specially Appearing Cross-Defendant Suzie Thorn To Set Aside Default
rulings.Rulings: Law and Motion Calendar for, Wednesday, October 15, 2014, line 4. Motion Of Specially Appearing Cross-Defendant Suzie Thorn To Set Aside Default.Granted. The default is set aside. Defendant has demonstrated that but for a clerical error, she would have filed a Motion to Quash before her time to answer the cross-complaint had expired. "The doctrine of relief in equity from mistake has applied where the mistake is that of the clerk of the court." (Baske v. Burke (1981) 125 Cal.App.3d 38, 44.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: TIMOTHY CAUWELS et al VS. DANA JOHNSON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion And Notice Of Motion For Order Compelling Discovery Responses; Request For Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, October 15, 2014, Line 6 - PLAINTIFF TIMOTHY CAUWELS and LESLIE CAUWELS' Motion For Order Compelling Discovery Responses; Request For Production Of Documents. Pro Tem Judge Peter Catalonotti, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Motion is GRANTED. As to RFP 4, all objections are overruled. Defendant DTG shall provide further amended responses omitting these objections and produce unredacted versions of documents marked DTG271-273 as well as the January 24, 2012 rental contract. As to RFP 18, the relevancy and unduly burdensome objections are overruled. The overbroad objection is sustained. The parties shall meet and confer prior to the hearing on limits on time and location (ie within 5 years of the incident and out of the San Francisco location). DTG shall provide a code-compliant response to RFP 18. The Court notes that no privacy objection was raised in either response to RFP 4 or 18. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to pcc@manningllp.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: GK CONSULTANTS LLC VS. JAMES P. DEMARCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER
rulings.Rulings: Law and Motion Calendar for, Wednesday, October 15, 2014, line 9. Plaintiff GK Consultants LLC's Application and Heqaring For Right To Attach Order. Denied. Pursuant to CCP sec. 483.010(c), a writ of attachment may only be issued against a natural person if the claim arises out of the defendant's trade, business, or profession. Defendant has not met its burden of establishing that the claim that is the basis of the writ of attachment meets this requirement. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: HERMANPREET SINGH VS. MERIDIAN BUILDERS & DEVELOPERS, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss And Transfer Venue
rulings.Rulings: Law and Motion Calendar for, Wednesday, October 15, 2014, line 10. Defendant Homeaway, Inc.'s Motion To Dismiss And Transfer Venue. Denied. Defendant Homeaway's Terms and Conditions were agreed to by the individual (referred to as "you" in the Terms and Conditions) who rented the house, not plaintiff. The Terms and Conditons included a forum selection clause that provides Travis County, Texas is the exclusive forum for "any cause of action you may submit in connection with your use of the site or pursuant to these terms." Plaintff, however, was not a signatory to the contract. (Declaration of Courtney Dickey, Ex. A.) Plaintiff is not so "closely related to the contractual relationship" that the forum selection clause should apply. (See Net2Phone, Inc. v. Sup. Ct. (2003) 109 Cal.App.4th 583, 587.) The cases relied on by Defendant are distinguishable. See, e.g., Net2Phone, supra, 109 Cal. App. 4th at 589 ( Net2Phone suing in a representative capacity and purporting to assert rights of the parties to the contract; it "stood in the shoes of those whom it purports to represent" and was bound by the forum selection clause); Manetti-Farrow, Inc. v. Gucci America, Inc. 858 F.2d 509, 514 n.5 (9th Cir. 1988) (non-signatory Gucci defendants could enforce forum selection clause where conduct of all Gucci defendants (including signatory Gucci Parfums) engaged in conduct that was closely related to contractual relationship); Lu v. Dryclean-USA of California, Inc. (1992) 11 Cal. App. 4th 1490, 1494) (non-signatory defendants alleged to be alter egos of signatory defendant could enforce forum selection clause; "to hold otherwise would be to permit a plaintiff to sidestep a valid forum selection clause simply by naming a closely related party who did not sign the clause as a defendant"). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)
